channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 🕵️ PHOTOS: The sights of Berlin Air Show 2026 on Day 1 | Breaking Defense
item.description: The Berlin Air Show has officially kicked off, with plenty to see.

item.title: 📍 Dusting a Dirt Road: How The United States Can Break the Cycle of Failing Military Infrastructure | War on the Rocks
item.description: Winter Storm Uri ripped through Texas in January 2021. The frigid temperatures froze pipes, which then burst and caused flooding in aging barracks at Fort Hood, many of which were overdue for renovations and had vulnerable mechanical and utility systems. The burst pipes, damaged…

item.title: 🔐 Cascading Failure: The Spanish Navy’s Reserve Squadron and the Tragedy of Unpreparedness | War on the Rocks
item.description: It is in long periods of peace that victories and defeats are prepared. Peace is the period for men of government, just as war is for men of action. Without the calm preparation carried out by the former, the courage and energy of the latter are of no use. — Captain First Class…

item.title: 📍 Lockheed pitches HIMARS for France, with 18-month timeline offer | Breaking Defense
item.description: France has allocated approximately €600 million ($692 million) to replace its legacy LRU launchers.

item.title: 📍 How to lose friends and alienate allies: Trump’s approach to Oman | ASPI Strategist
item.description: President Donald Trump’s 27 May threat to ‘blow up’ Oman if it didn’t fall into line was strategically self-defeating. With one off-the-cuff remark, the United States risked damaging the one quiet, trusted channel through which ...

item.title: 🛡️ Why Cambodia and Thailand’s Peace Is So Uneasy | Foreign Policy
item.description: A step forward in the maritime dispute masks dangers on the land border.

item.title: 🏴 Turkey’s Quiet Realignment | Foreign Affairs
item.description: Russia’s loss is NATO’s gain.

item.title: 🏴 The End of the Open Internet | Foreign Affairs
item.description: Europe lost the plot on online speech.

item.title: 🌏 The Fault Lines in China’s Power | Foreign Affairs
item.description: America must build—and use—leverage against Beijing.

item.title: 📍 ‘A terrible risk’: Senate appropriators dim prospects of another defense reconciliation bill | Defense One
item.description: Trump’s shipbuilding, munitions, and Golden Dome efforts rely on extra-budgetary funding maneuver.

item.title: 🌏 China isn’t defending critical minerals investment. It’s defending leverage | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Chinese reactions to Treasurer Jim Chalmers’s decision to order six China-linked investors to divest their holdings in Northern Minerals tells us far more than the decision itself. China’s Global Times publication condemned the move as ...

item.title: 📍 The global energy demand era calls for major change—here’s how countries are pivoting | Atlantic Council
item.description: Energy experts, policymakers, and business leaders at the Atlantic Council's tenth Global Energy Forum share their insights into the current crisis and the path toward a more energy secure future. The post The global energy demand era calls for major change—here’s how countries…

item.title: 🌍 U.S. Launches New ‘Self-Defense’ Strikes on Iran Over Apache Helicopter Crash | Foreign Policy
item.description: But Iranian officials reportedly claim that the downing was not deliberate.

item.title: 🌍 AH-64 Apache Shot Down By Iran, U.S. Will Retaliate: Trump (Updated) | The War Zone
item.description: The AH-64 crashed into the Gulf of Oman and its crew was rescued by a drone boat, and now Trump says it was due to enemy action. The post AH-64 Apache Shot Down By Iran, U.S. Will Retaliate: Trump (Updated) appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 US launches new strikes on Iran after helicopter downed | Defense News
item.description: The U.S. launched strikes against Iran on Tuesday after Trump said Tehran had shot down a U.S. Apache helicopter in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: 🌏 Xi Seeks to Draw North Korea Back Into China’s Orbit | Foreign Policy
item.description: The Chinese leader’s long-awaited visit to Pyongyang aims to counter Russian influence.

item.title: 📍 How global authoritarians sustain inequality | Atlantic Council
item.description: Authoritarian leaders across the world use targeted strategies to sustain inequality. But developing countries should instead pull from a variety of existing development models to create societal prosperity. The post How global authoritarians sustain inequality appeared first on…

item.title: 📍 Space Force contracts Viasat, Intelsat for first of new anti-jam communication sats | Breaking Defense
item.description: The two awards are collectively worth $437.7 million, according to the Space Systems Command press release.

item.title: 🌍 US Energy Secretary Chris Wright: It will take ‘many months’ to get back to normal after this energy crisis | Atlantic Council
item.description: At the tenth annual Global Energy Forum, Wright said that “the price” of this crisis “will be wildly worth paying,” if it means that Iran eventually ceases to be “a constant threat to its neighbors." The post US Energy Secretary Chris Wright: It will take ‘many months’ to get…

item.title: 📍 Can Northeast Asia’s New Geopolitical Reality Create Opportunities for Mongolia? | The Diplomat
item.description: Within the emerging geopolitical landscape, Mongolia may have opportunities to diversify its economic partners.

item.title: 🌍 In memo to CENTCOM leaders, Cooper warns of duty to protect civilians | Breaking Defense
item.description: “Our commitment to the law of armed conflict is not a constraint on our lethality,” Adm. Brad Cooper, the CENTCOM head, wrote in a memo.

item.title: 🏛️ Beyond submarines: AUKMIN is about collective defence of shared values | ASPI Strategist
item.description: AUKUS is likely to dominate coverage of this week’s meetings between Australian and British foreign and defence ministers (AUKMIN) in London. All four ministers should use the opportunity to take back the narrative on the ...

item.title: 🏛️ New calls for lawmakers to override Trump’s anti-union EO at the Pentagon | Defense One
item.description: Last year, the Senate blocked a House measure to halt ban on collective bargaining at the Defense Department and other agencies.

item.title: 🏛️ This Is The Corsair Drone Boat That Plucked The Downed Apache Crew Out Of The Gulf Of Oman | The War Zone
item.description: The U.S. Navy's top drone task force in the Middle East only started operating the Corsair uncrewed surface vessels in March. The post This Is The Corsair Drone Boat That Plucked The Downed Apache Crew Out Of The Gulf Of Oman appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌏 Why a South Korean Starbucks Ad Mocked Murdered Protesters | Foreign Policy
item.description: Far-right meme politics shows how strong anti-democratic forces remain.

item.title: 🏴 Inside Ukraine’s AI-Enabled Drone Campaign Targeting Russian Logistics Deep Behind The Lines | The War Zone
item.description: An officer assigned to the "mid-range" drone campaign gives unprecedented insights into its strikes at the heart of Russian logistics. The post Inside Ukraine’s AI-Enabled Drone Campaign Targeting Russian Logistics Deep Behind The Lines appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🛡️ Coast Guard, Saildrone team up in northern waters to boost border security, surveillance | Breaking Defense
item.description: The Voyager is equipped to conduct persistent coastal surveillance and nearshore mapping missions while remaining at sea for roughly 100 days at a time.

item.title: 🏛️ In apparent first, Navy drone boat rescues helicopter crew downed at sea | Defense One
item.description: The unmanned vessel, made by Texas-based Saronic, was sent to the region in March.

item.title: 🔐 New CISA directive will reshape how agencies prioritize cyber risks, official says | Defense One
item.description: Federal government will be directed to plan first to ward off the most devastating attacks.

item.title: 🏴 NATO’s Digital Back End Could Fall Apart Without Change | Foreign Policy
item.description: Shared standards on cloud services are urgently needed.
